conclusion: Full frontier-model fixed-weight physicalization remains rejected absent evidence closing update, yield, integration, and programmable-baseline burdens.
The safety/filter hybrid wins zero Phase 2 workload scenarios against the stronger programmable baseline.
The hybrid architecture remains useful for interface, fallback, audit, update, and failure-mode reasoning, not as a current winner.
The fixed classifier prototype has bounded combinational closure evidence; compiled Verilator remains blocked by unavailable build tools.
Future restart uses the existing Phase 4 conjunction; no current artifact is an actual reopen candidate.
M-ROBUST-1 found zero calibrated physicalized wins across target classes; favorable-plausible wins are not current evidence.
The campaign is closed under current evidence and defers future work unless real lifecycle-valid measured evidence appears.
